title: Level 600
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 600: "Deathly Desert"}} {{Class Deadzone}} {{Blurimage|file=Level606New4.jpg|caption=A recovered image of Level 600. Photographer deceased.|hovertext=They lurk beneath the sands...}} ==Description== '''Level 600''', informally known as the 'Deathly Desert' or the 'Desert of Death', is a highly dangerous level of the Backrooms. It consists of an expansive desert mostly dominated by dunes of varying sizes, reminiscent of those found in [[The Frontrooms]]. However, unlike typical deserts, the desert that makes up this level's landscape is believed to be much larger; this is evidenced by the fact that no other environments or bodies of water have ever been discovered in '''Level 600''', rendering its expanse completely desolate and uninhabitable for most forms of life, including humans. However, the possibility of there being something other than the aforementioned desert remains. The temperature of '''Level 600''' remains at an arid 35°C (95°F). Additionally, no weather events, such as rainfall or thunderstorms, have ever been reported, although the relatively normal amount of H2O present in both the air and sand would suggest that rain has indeed visited the level. Researchers have hypothesized that an unknown property exclusive to '''Level 600''' is keeping the level hydrated, albeit still as dry as a regular desert; further supporting this theory is wanderers having never spotted any clouds. It is a presumably similar anomaly that generated the level's dunes given the lack of wind. No sun has ever been spotted either, though the level nonetheless remains in a constant state of day. Illogically-located shadows are sparsely present throughout '''Level 600''' despite the fact that these areas are evidently not shielded from the light of the sky. The angles at which these shadows are cast are seemingly inconsistent and nonsensical, with different shadows not following any set angle like regular shadows would. Humanoid skeletons can very rarely be found partially or completely buried under the desert's sands. Some of these remains look normal, while others have random and illogical aberrations such as fused joints or abnormally shaped bones. These anomalies have raised suspicions as to whether all skeletons found in the level were simply always there rather than originating from actual life. The survival rate of human life in '''Level 600''' past 1 hour is 0%, and therefore, one should never attempt to enter the level. However, if one finds themselves in this level, they should immediately seek an exit before one of the entities here kills them. ==Entities== Even despite the level's heat anomalies, the main danger presented by '''Level 600''' is its level-exclusive entities, which pose a significant threat to anyone in the level at all times. ===Death Worms=== Death Worms, scientifically referred to as ''Mors Vermis'', are one of the two level-exclusive entities that inhabit '''Level 600''', and are known to infest the level underneath the surface, occasionally 'jumping' out of the sand and briefly arcing in the air above, before diving straight down into the sands again and continuing their silent and unseen prowl. They are common within the level and one is bound to draw the attention of at least one of them as soon as they enter the level. As their name implies, Death Worms bear a resemblance to regular worms, but they are significantly larger, often stretching as long as five houses combined and as wide as an average man. Their mouths are large and circular, making up their entire face; these mouths are ringed with multiple rows of sharp, curved teeth for chewing and eating prey. Death Worms also possess a hard, thick hide capable of stopping bullets from getting into the inner body. Not much else is known about Death Worms due to their inherent danger, but it is assumed that they possess a basic organ structure at least somewhat reminiscent of that of a regular worm. {{Blurimage|file=Level606New2.jpg|caption=An image of Level 600. Photographer alive.|hovertext=They could appear at any time...}} ===Death Birds=== Death Birds, scientifically referred to as ''Mors Avis'', are the second type of level-exclusive entity that inhabits '''Level 600''', and although they are less common than Death Worms, they are still very dangerous. They are often not initially visible to wanderers in the level, despite them being known to inhabit the sky, although if one survives long enough in the level, a Death Bird will likely inexplicably appear in the sky and start swooping down to attack the wanderer. There is little one can do to defend against a Death Bird attack, and most are either captured or killed during the first swoop. Physically, Death Birds appear to be large, monstrous, bird-like creatures somewhat reminiscent of falconiform birds of prey. Their main body is about as large as an adult elephant from the [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]], with a wingspan about three times as wide as the average adult man is tall. Their tails measure nearly as long as their bodies, and their backs and wings are adorned with spikes intermingled with their feathers. However, despite the spikes' apparent purpose, Death Birds have never been observed to use these spikes to stab, slash, or otherwise injure prey, instead only using their beaks and talons to bite and slash, respectively, at said prey. It has been theorized that they do not utilize these spikes because they simply do not need them, though it is also possible that the spikes are not naturally designed to inflict damage and instead have another use. Still, what this use may be is currently unknown. ==Colonies & Outposts== Due to the harsh nature of '''Level 600''' and the nonexistent survivability past 1 hour for anyone who enters the level, no colonies nor outposts were able to be established and likely never will be unless some form of revolutionary technology equipped for the task is invented. {{Blurimage|file=Level606.jpg|caption=Another recovered image of Level 600. Photographer deceased.|hovertext=Escape or die.}} ==Entrances & Exits== ===Entrances=== * Very rarely, one may find themselves here after waking up from an anomalous dream in [[Level 600.1]]. * Noclipping through the sand in [[Level 671]] will take one here. ===Exits=== * Noclipping into a piece of asphalt road that uncommonly appears partially buried under some sand dunes will send one to [[Level 91]]. * Wandering for long enough may take one to [[Level 600.1]]. * Noclipping through the sand below the surface if it is exposed will take one to [[Level 100]]. ---- {{Collapse|num=2|initial= '''Open Author & Credits Info''' |final= '''Close Author & Credits Info''' |body= '''Author:''' [[User:Forknife468152|Forknife]] '''Image 1:''' Combination of images from [https://www.pinterest.com.au/ Pinterest] and [https://www.vecteezy.com/ Vecteezy], edited by me
